title: Upon A Wife That Died Mad With Jealousy.
content:
In this little vault she lies,
    Here, with all her jealousies:
    Quiet yet; but if ye make
    Any noise they both will wake,
    And such spirits raise 'twill then
    Trouble death to lay again.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon A Wife That Died Mad With Jealousy."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
